notes: Run 2026-06-20. Gaps filled: Architecture (Sagrada Família, Parthenon), Art Movements (Baroque), Art Terms & Techniques (Silhouette), Public Art & Monuments (Winged Victory), Museums (Uffizi, Hermitage), Painters (Cézanne, O'Keeffe, Rivera, Gainsborough). Three visual clues. Added first $1000 to this category.

questions.clue: Depicting a stern, pitchfork-holding farmer and his daughter before their white, steep-gabled Iowa farmhouse, this 1930 Grant Wood oil painting is among the most widely parodied images in the nation's art.

citations.title: American Gothic
citations.snippet: American Gothic is a 1930 oil painting on beaverboard by the American Regionalist artist Grant Wood, depicting a Midwestern farmer and his daughter standing in front of their Carpenter Gothic style home. It is one of the most famous America

questions.clue: Flourishing from the early 1600s until the 1750s, this Western artistic style was actively promoted by the Catholic Church as a dramatic counter to the perceived austerity of Protestant art and architecture.

citations.title: Baroque
citations.snippet: The Baroque is a Western style of architecture, music, dance, painting, sculpture, poetry, and other arts that flourished from the early 1600s until the 1750s. It followed Renaissance art and Mannerism and preceded the Rococo and Neoclassic

questions.clue: Begun in 1882 and still unfinished, this Antoni Gaudí basilica in Barcelona's Eixample district is the tallest church in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

citations.title: Sagrada Família
citations.snippet: Basílica i Temple Expiatori de la Sagrada Família, or simply Sagrada Família, is a Catholic church under construction in the Eixample district of Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain. Designed by Catalan architect Antoni Gaudí, it is the tallest chu

questions.clue: This art form — representing a subject as a solid, featureless black profile against a contrasting background — became popular in the mid-18th century as an affordable alternative to the portrait miniature, with skilled practitioners able to cut a finished example purely by eye in minutes.

citations.title: Silhouette
citations.snippet: A silhouette is the image of a person, animal, object or scene represented as a solid shape of a single colour, usually black, with its edges matching the outline of the subject. The interior of a silhouette is featureless, and the silhouet

questions.clue: Adjacent to Florence's Piazza della Signoria, this museum holds one of Italy's most important Renaissance collections; when the Medici dynasty died out, the last heiress bequeathed their entire art collection to the city of Florence under a 'family pact.'

citations.title: Uffizi
citations.snippet: The Uffizi Gallery is a prominent art museum adjacent to the Piazza della Signoria in the Historic Centre of Florence in the region of Tuscany, Italy. One of the most important Italian museums and the most visited, it is also one of the lar

questions.clue: Called the 'Mother of American modernism,' this painter is best known for large-scale close-up images of flowers and for the desert landscapes she painted around Abiquiú, New Mexico, where she eventually settled permanently.

citations.title: Georgia O'Keeffe
citations.snippet: Georgia Totto O'Keeffe was an American modernist painter and draftswoman whose career spanned seven decades and whose work remained largely independent of major art movements. Called the "Mother of American modernism", O'Keeffe gained inter

questions.clue: Norwegian artist Edvard Munch created four versions of this 1893 composition, each depicting an anguished figure clutching its face against a turbulent orange sky.

citations.title: The Scream
citations.snippet: The Scream is an art composition created by Norwegian artist Edvard Munch in 1893. The Norwegian name of the piece is Skrik ('Scream'), and the German title under which it was first exhibited is Der Schrei der Natur. The agonized face in th

questions.clue: This circa 190 BC Hellenistic marble statue of the goddess Nike, mounted on a base shaped like a ship's prow, has stood at the top of a grand Parisian staircase since 1884; its head and arms are missing.

citations.title: Winged Victory of Samothrace
citations.snippet: The Winged Victory of Samothrace, or the Nike of Samothrace, is a votive monument originally discovered on the island of Samothrace in the northeastern Aegean Sea. It is a masterpiece of Greek sculpture from the Hellenistic era, dating from

questions.clue: Founded in 1764 when Empress Catherine the Great purchased a Berlin merchant's collection of Dutch and Flemish paintings, this Saint Petersburg institution holds the largest collection of paintings in the world.

citations.title: Hermitage Museum
citations.snippet: The State Hermitage Museum is a museum of art and culture in Saint Petersburg, Russia, and holds the largest collection of paintings in the world. It was founded in 1764 when Empress Catherine the Great acquired a collection of paintings fr

questions.clue: Both Henri Matisse and Pablo Picasso are said to have called this French Post-Impressionist painter 'the father of us all'; his geometric studies of Mont Sainte-Victoire near his hometown of Aix-en-Provence bridged 19th-century Impressionism and 20th-century Cubism.

citations.title: Paul Cézanne
citations.snippet: Paul Cézanne was a French Post-Impressionist painter whose work introduced new modes of representation, influenced avant-garde artistic movements of the early 20th century and formed the bridge between late 19th-century Impressionism and ea

questions.clue: Between 1922 and 1953, this Mexican painter covered walls in Mexico City, San Francisco, Detroit, and New York with large frescoes, helping establish the mural movement in Mexican and international art; his third wife was fellow Mexican artist Frida Kahlo.

citations.title: Diego Rivera
citations.snippet: Diego María de la Concepción Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodríguez was a Mexican painter. His large frescoes helped establish the mural movement in Mexican and international art.

questions.clue: During a 1687 siege, Venetian artillery struck this building — which the occupying Ottomans had converted into a gunpowder magazine — triggering an explosion that split its central colonnade and severely damaged a structure that had already stood for over 2,000 years.

citations.title: Parthenon
citations.snippet: The Parthenon is a former temple on the Athenian Acropolis, Greece, that was dedicated to the goddess Athena. Its decorative sculptures are considered some of the high points of classical Greek art, and the Parthenon is considered an enduri

citations.title: Impression, Sunrise
citations.snippet: Impression, Sunrise is an 1872 painting by Claude Monet first shown at what would become known as the "Exhibition of the Impressionists" in Paris in April, 1874. The painting is credited with inspiring the name of the Impressionist movement

citations.title: Las Meninas
citations.snippet: Las Meninas is a 1656 painting in the Museo del Prado in Madrid, by Diego Velázquez, the leading artist in the court of King Philip IV of Spain and Portugal, and of the Spanish Golden Age. It has become one of the most widely analyzed works

citations.title: The Blue Boy
